scope: Compact field dictionary and validation summary for the local mooncat_traits.json upstream reference snapshot.

datasetShape.rowSemantics: one observed row per MoonCat in rescueOrder array order

requiredFields.meaning: Array-backed rescue order index for the row.
requiredFields.identifierNotes: Aligned with array index in the checked local snapshot. See data/identifier-conventions.json before using this with API or token-facing identifiers.

requiredFields.meaning: Observed rescue year value in the upstream reference row.

requiredFields.identifierNotes: This is the bytes5 MoonCat ID form. Conversion to rescue order is array-backed by the reference snapshot, not a closed-form formula.

requiredFields.meaning: Observed numeric hue value in the upstream reference row.
Observed hue label in the upstream reference row.
Observed pale/coating boolean in the upstream reference row.

optionalFields.meaning: Observed genesis marker in rows where present.
limitations: Absence is not normalized here into false.

optionalFields.meaning: Observed raw name value in rows where present.
limitations: Name strings are not imported into this schema summary.

optionalFields.meaning: Observed normalized or marker-style name field in rows where present.
limitations: Name values and exact semantics are not curated in this pass.

optionalFields.meaning: Observed name-order field in rows where present.
limitations: Exact name event semantics are not curated in this pass.

optionalFields.meaning: Observed name-year field in rows where present.
limitations: Exact name event semantics are not curated in this pass.

optionalFields.meaning: Observed accessory-count-like field in rows where present.
limitations: Freshness and update cadence are not verified in this pass.
Accessory ownership details are not imported here.

supportingSourceRefs.use: supporting context for array-backed identifier lookup helpers; not a source for importing per-cat rows here
supporting contract surface context for future trait accessor review; not a full imported trait mapping here
supporting API shape context for future endpoint compatibility checks; not used here to claim response schemas

curatedTraitModelDecision.summary: A deterministic 64-row representative visual-trait prototype now tests the model with explicit source priority, per-field provenance categories, identifier round trips, and mismatch reporting; full-population import remains undecided.

notes: Keep references/upstream/mooncatrescue/mooncat_traits.json as reference input, not curated KB data.
Use scripts/generate-visual-traits.py and scripts/validate-visual-traits.py rather than hand-editing per-cat rows.
See docs/generated-trait-data.md for source priority, selection, mismatch, and scaling decisions.

preferredIdentifierKey.rationale: bytes5 catId is the MoonCat-native identifier and remains stable across token-facing wrapper conventions.
preferredIdentifierKey.secondaryUse: Allowed as a lookup/index field only when explicitly sourced from array order or the upstream row and checked against data/identifier-conventions.json.

allowedFields.requirement: allowed if sourced from the same validated row
allowed as normalized visual traits when value sets and optional-field handling are validated
required at artifact level and allowed at field group level when sources differ
allowed only as separately versioned derived display metadata; preserve raw hueInt, hueName, pale, and genesis and record source-backed policy provenance
required method metadata for generated artifacts
required validation metadata for generated artifacts

disallowedFields.reason: Name-related fields have different freshness and event semantics; keep reference-only until a separate provenance and update-cadence decision is made.
Accessory-related data may change or depend on current ownership surfaces; keep reference-only until a separate accessory data model exists.
These require separate generated-data or domain-specific passes and should not be copied into curated per-cat trait rows by default.

requiredProvenanceAndMethod: Use sourceRefs registered in data/sources.json before relying on external or upstream references.
Record the exact source snapshot or live source surface, generation command or script, checked row count, identifier alignment checks, and optional-field normalization policy.
Document whether the generated artifact is complete, partial, stale-risk, or update-cadence-bound.
If sources conflict, document the conflict rather than silently choosing one source.
validationStrategy: Check one row per included catId and no duplicate catId values.
Check rescueOrder range and uniqueness for any complete dataset.
Check rescueOrder alignment against array index or documented lookup method.
Check required visual trait fields and allowed value sets.
Check optional genesis handling explicitly rather than converting absence silently.
Check every sourceRef resolves through data/sources.json.
Keep aggregate reports separate unless the pass explicitly creates derived summaries.
openQuestions: Whether a future artifact should include all 25,440 cats or retain a smaller generated lookup optimized for common KB tasks.
Whether a full generated artifact should be committed directly, sharded, or produced only at build time.
Whether visual trait fields should be versioned separately from API/name/accessory-related fields.
What freshness policy should apply if current API or ChainStation-maintained trait artifacts diverge from the local upstream snapshot.
Whether any consumer needs rescueOrder-primary shape despite bytes5 catId being the preferred primary key.

limitations: This file is schema and validation metadata, not the full 25,440-row trait dataset.
Only 64 representative per-cat rows are imported; names, accessory details, and frequency tables remain excluded.
Observed value sets describe the checked local reference snapshot and are not independently claimed as a canonical trait dictionary.
The local snapshot copied or retrieved date is not recorded in the reference README.
Freshness of API, ChainStation, name, and accessory-related data is not resolved here.
todos: Decide whether names and accessory-related fields need separate provenance or update cadence before any curated import.
If needed, create a separate generated validation report with aggregate counts and a reproducible command.
Decide whether the bounded prototype should ever scale to a full generated population artifact.
